headd: Objective Test Questions: Answer all the questions

S1.headd: In each of the following sentences there is one word or group of words underlined and one gap. From the list of words or group fo words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word or group of words and that will at the same time, correctly fill the gap in the sentence.

S1.qs: 1.	To encourage productivity we must **reward** industry and .................. laziness
2.	I took the drug to **relieve** my headache only for it to ..................... my condition
3.	**An optimist** wears a smile most of the time but ...................... goes about with a long face
4.	You will make a good counsellor by **listening** to your clients rather than ............. them
5.	Tell Chike something **confidential** and he makes it ................. the next minute
6.	You will succeed fairly well with adolescents if you **coax** rather than .................. them
7.	While Adioa is **attentive** to her duties, Kofi is ...............
8.	Life has become a **misery** for many due to the harsh economic conditions, but a few are still able to live in .............

S2.headd: From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that best completes each of the following sentences

S2.qs: 9.	He is a young man of .................. character
10.	My parents do not ................ late night parties
11.	I have known him only recently so I cannot ................ for his honesty
12.	In some cultures, dead people are buried, but in others they are	........................
14.	It is bad manners to regard a stranger as an ...............
15.	In some countries, it is an offence to describe a child born out of wedlock as ....................
16.	In hazy weather, air travel is ...............
17.	He has since taken over the ................. of power
18.	The government has promised to ............... the road construction project

S3.headd: After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.

S3.qs: 19.	I am sure you didn’t know that the lady is light-fingered. This means that the lady is
20.	Unfortunately nowadays most young persons do not see eye to eye with their parents over matters of friendship and fashion. This means that parent and their children

opts.q0: A. can’t look one another in the face
opts.q1: B. hold strong view on friendship and fashion
opts.q2: C. have clothes different from their friends
opts.q3: D. hold different views about friendship and fashion

S3.qs: 21.	It is alleged that the accountants doctored the accounts of the company. This means that the accountants

opts.q0: A. called in the company doctor
opts.q2: C. became sick on account of the bribe they received
opts.q3: D. improved the accounts of the company

S3.qs: 22.	He has just been made the new Chief Executive so he will need some time to learn the ropes. This means that with time he will

opts.q0: A. fix new gadgets in his new office
opts.q1: B. clear the abandoned things on the site
opts.q2: C. master the demands of his new office
opts.q3: D. learn the use of ropes in his new office

S3.qs: 23.	After the investigation many of the workers were given their marching orders. This means that the workers

opts.q2: C. received fresh instructions about their job
opts.q3: D. were given orders similar to the first ones

S3.qs: 24.	In his last days in office, the ruler was behaving like a bull in a China shop. This means that the ruler

opts.q0: A. loved the bull because he brought it from China
opts.q1: B. behaved in a manner that pleased the people
opts.q2: C. did not want to leave office
opts.q3: D. was acting in a manner unsuitable for his position

S3.qs: 25.	Before all the election results were announced it was clear that the leader of the opposition was home and dry. This means that the leader of the opposition

opts.q3: D. has arrived home before the rain

S3.qs: 26.	Whenever you discuss nuclear physics Ali is in his elements. This means that Ali

opts.q0: A. does not like nuclear physics
opts.q1: B. hates people who discuss nuclear physics
opts.q3: D. knows that nuclear physics is difficult

S3.qs: 27.	I am not going to say yes or no until you come out into the open. This means that I will not give an answer until

opts.q1: B. we agree on a position
opts.q3: D. you reveal your own position

S3.qs: 28.	We shall understand him better by and by. This means that we shall know him better

opts.q2: C. by getting closer to him

30.	I went through his work, it was all **gibberish**

33.	His contribution to the development of the hospital remains **unrivalled**
34.	I have a feeling that he is doing some **shady** business outside the country
35.	The decision I have taken is **irrevocable**
36.	Young children seldom engage in **gainful** activities
37.	The bank that started operations last year is now **insolvent**
